006package tools.PathplannerSearch;
007
008import java.io.IOException;
009import tools.PathplannerSearch.PathplannerSearch.SearchType;
010
011/**
012 *
013 *
014 * <h2>Main class for PathplannerSearch</h2>
015 *
016 * <p>
017 * To use, run:
018 *
019 * <pre>
020 * ./gradlew runPathplannerSearch -Pargs="[search term] [search type]"
021 * </pre>
022 *
023 * <p>
024 * <b>Example:</b>
025 *
026 * <pre>
027 * ./gradlew runPathplannerSearch -Pargs="disruptAUton path"
028 * </pre>
029 *
030 * If your search term has spaces, it will only check the last word for the
031 * search type.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * <b>Example:</b>
035 *
036 * <pre>
037 * ./gradlew runPathplannerSearch -Pargs="Disrupt Auton Thing path"
038 * </pre>
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * <b>Arguments:</b>
042 *
043 * <ul>
044 * <li><b>[search term]</b>: The term to search for in the Pathplanner files.
045 * <li><b>[search type]</b>: The type of search to perform:
046 * <ul>
047 * <li>{@code linked_waypoint}
048 * <li>{@code path}
049 * </ul>
050 * </ul>
051 */
052public class Main {
053    private enum Arguments {
054        SEARCH_TERM,
055        SEARCH_TYPE;
056    }
057
058    public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException {
059        String searchTerm = args[Arguments.SEARCH_TERM.ordinal()] != null
060                ? args[Arguments.SEARCH_TERM.ordinal()].toLowerCase()
061                : "";
062        SearchType searchType = args[Arguments.SEARCH_TYPE.ordinal()] != null
063                ? SearchType.valueOf(args[Arguments.SEARCH_TYPE.ordinal()].toUpperCase())
064                : SearchType.PATH;
065
066        PathplannerSearch.search(searchTerm, searchType);
067    }
068}
